title: Note download in read view
motivation: Readers viewing a note have no way to save it locally — the only actions in the read-view toolbar are Edit and Delete. Users want to export the note they are reading as a portable file so they can keep, share, or back up its content outside the app. The export is a pure client-side download of the markdown already loaded into the read view, requiring no new backend route or data fetch.

included: A download control in the read-view action toolbar that saves the currently-viewed note as a markdown file, generated entirely in the browser from already-loaded note data.
excluded: HTML or PDF export — download is raw markdown only.
Any new backend endpoint or server round-trip — the feature is pure client-side from loaded note data.
Including the H1 title in the file body — the title is carried only in the filename, and the body is the title-stripped markdown the read view renders.

design_facts.decision: Download is raw markdown as a .md file; no HTML or PDF export.
design_facts.rationale: The note is authored and rendered as markdown; raw markdown is the lossless portable form.

design_facts.decision: Filename is the slugified note title plus the .md extension (e.g. 'Meeting Notes 6/10' becomes meeting-notes-6-10.md), filesystem-safe on all platforms, falling back to the note id when the title slugifies to empty.
design_facts.rationale: A human-readable, filesystem-safe name lets users identify the file without the title living inside the body; the id fallback guarantees a valid filename for degenerate titles.

design_facts.decision: The downloaded body is the title-stripped markdown the read view renders after removeH1FromContent, not the full note content; the title lives in the filename only.
design_facts.rationale: Mirrors what the reader sees rendered and avoids duplicating the title in both filename and body.

design_facts.decision: An icon button with a download icon, matching the existing Delete IconButton pattern in the read-view action toolbar, placed alongside Edit and Delete.
design_facts.rationale: Visual and interaction consistency with the established toolbar controls.

contract.filename_rule: slugify(note.title) + '.md'; filesystem-safe on all platforms (e.g. 'Meeting Notes 6/10' → 'meeting-notes-6-10.md'). When the title slugifies to empty (empty/whitespace-only title), fall back to the note id: 'note-<id>.md'. 'Untitled Note' slugifies normally (untitled-note.md).
contract.file_body_source: removeH1FromContent(note.content) — the title-stripped markdown the read view renders; NOT the full note.content
contract.control: download IconButton with a download icon, matching the existing Delete IconButton pattern in NoteActions
contract.placement: read-view action toolbar, alongside Edit and Delete
contract.generation: pure client-side; file generated in the browser from already-loaded note data; no backend route, no fetch

assertions: Read view of a note renders a download control (an icon button with a download icon) in the action toolbar alongside Edit and Delete.
Activating the download control triggers a browser file download whose filename is the slugified note title plus '.md' (e.g. a note titled 'Meeting Notes 6/10' downloads as 'meeting-notes-6-10.md').
The downloaded file body equals removeH1FromContent(note.content) — the title-stripped markdown — and does NOT contain the H1 title line.
Downloading performs no network request — the file is built from the note data already loaded into the read view.
A note whose title slugifies to empty (e.g. whitespace-only) downloads as 'note-<id>.md' using the note's id.
oracle.setup: A logged-in user viewing a note in the read (non-editing) view, with the note already present in loader data.
oracle.tooling: Vitest + React Testing Library with render from test-utils/render; mock or assert on the browser download mechanism (anchor click / object URL).
